title: narcissus abscissus
intro: Narcissus abscissus, also known as the false garlic, is a perennial plant that belongs to the Amaryllidaceae family, characterized by its underground bulb and fragrant leaves. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include its antispasmodic, diaphoretic, and expectorant properties, helping to relieve symptoms of coughs, colds, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Narcissus abscissus include its ability to act as an expectorant, helping to clear mucus from the lungs, and its antispasmodic properties, which can help to relax the muscles and reduce inflammation. Herbal preparations of Narcissus abscissus include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, including bronchitis, asthma, and coughs.
